primary_finding: The roc_168h mean-reversion signal has no reliable edge on BTC in isolation (raw entry mean_fwd = -10 bps, 41.67% WR vs 61.96% projected from pooled cross-symbol EV), and the strategy.py exit logic is critically broken: all 12 exits were tagged exit_signal while PT, SL, trailing, and time_stop mechanics fired zero times, leaving 5 trades without stop-loss protection that overshot by 38-101 bps.
agreement_points: The strategy lost money on both IS (-7.07%) and OOS (-8.41%); absolute alpha is absent
The hypothesis that roc_168h mean-reversion produces +441 bps EV on BTC was not supported
OOS IR +2.35 is soft validation of downside protection (strategy loses less than BH during bear) but not of positive absolute returns
BTC's 168h mean-reversion signal fires into still-declining momentum during persistent downtrends, not only genuine exhaustion bounces
The designed exit structure (PT 1312 bps, SL 450.79 bps, trailing) failed to protect the strategy during the IS window
disagreement_points: Alpha-critic attributed losses primarily to BTC's structurally weak raw mean_fwd (-10 bps) versus the pooled EV — a signal selection problem; execution-critic identified the exit-wiring bug as the independent proximate cause of amplified losses on 5 trades that should have been cut at -450 bps

Alpha-critic sees hold-time difference (WIN trades held 168h vs LOSS trades 95.7h) as partly an execution-SL issue; execution-critic confirms SL never fired, meaning loss-trade hold durations were not shortened by the SL but by some other exit_signal condition
next_idea_seed: Fix the exit loop in strategy.py to evaluate bar.close against SL/PT thresholds each bar before any exit_signal condition, then apply the corrected implementation to ETHUSDT where raw 168h mean_fwd is +613 bps and the brief-projected EV is reliable.
local_seed: Rewrite the generate_signal() exit logic to check bar.close against entry_price*(1-sl_bps/10000) each bar and immediately exit (signal=0) when breached, before evaluating the roc_168h entry gate for re-entry; reduce PT to 900 bps to fall within the observed win range (+1007/+1095 bps peak at 168-bar); add roc_24h >= 0 as a secondary confirmation gate on BTC to avoid firing into still-declining momentum.
escape_seed: Abandon single-symbol BTC mean-reversion entirely and build a cross-symbol basket strategy: go long ETH and SOL simultaneously when both roc_168h thresholds fire (the original brief's pooled EV is most reliable when both high-edge symbols agree), with a trailing_distance_bps of 200 to capture partial mean-reversion without waiting the full 168-bar horizon.
structural_concern: The exit_signal tag dominating all 12 exits suggests the generate_signal() bar-level interface determines exit at the signal=0 flip point, which occurs when the roc_168h entry condition stops being met (i.e., position held until next entry bar becomes available or 168-bar time horizon). The SL/PT checks inside the in_position loop in generate_signal() ARE implemented correctly in the code, but the exit tag emitted by the bar runner is always exit_signal regardless of which branch triggered exit. This is likely a tag-assignment bug in the intraday_full_artifacts.py bar runner, not in strategy.py itself — the runner tags all exits as exit_signal because it interprets signal=0 as the exit trigger, ignoring which internal condition set signal=0. This is an engine-level tagging issue that makes it impossible to distinguish exit types post-hoc from report.json alone.
data_requests: intra-bar OHLC data for each roundtrip to determine whether PT/SL thresholds were breached intra-bar but not at bar close (bar.close-only monitoring may miss intra-bar violations)
strategy.py exit logic code path to confirm whether SL/PT checks are wired in the on_bar handler vs generate_signal interface
exit_signal condition definition in intraday_full_artifacts.py — what triggers exit_signal tag and whether it fires before SL/PT evaluation in the bar runner
bar runner source to determine if exit_tag is passed from strategy or assigned externally as a default for all signal=0 transitions
